role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for identifying the home health agency and managing all vendor communications regarding the intake coordinator's absence, while the Patient Navigator's role is limited to auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the transition process, and flagging the HHA acceptance delay to the Social Worker.
narrative_summary: A 72-year-old female recovering from an ORIF procedure for a hip fracture was medically cleared for discharge, but the transition was delayed when the home health agency's intake coordinator became unavailable due to an illness. This administrative gap left the family anxious about the timing of post-surgical physical therapy and nursing support at home. The Patient Navigator acted as a liaison by educating the family on the transition timeline and flagging the HHA stall to the Social Worker to ensure the home visit was not scheduled prematurely. Through consistent documentation in Atlantis and coordination with the Social Worker, the Navigator confirmed the agency's eventual acceptance before securing the first home visit.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record; discovered the patient's primary contact number was missing one digit. Verified the correct phone number and updated the patient profile in Atlantis to ensure the home team can reach the family for the pulse call.
v_card_flyer_status: Delivered the program flyer and successfully inserted the V-Card into the daughter's smartphone. Explained that the Caller ID will show 'Healing Partners' for all post-discharge monitoring calls to ensure the family recognizes the medical assistant's outreach.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pulse call with the daughter; the family's sentiment is currently 'Anxious' due to the uncertainty surrounding the HHA intake coordinator's return. Confirmed the home is prepared with the necessary DME, but the family is reluctant to discharge without a confirmed nursing visit time.
atlantis_final_sync: Recorded the discharge date of 10/24 and the first medical assistant visit date of 10/25 in Atlantis after the Social Worker confirmed the HHA had finally processed the referral.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Patient's profile appears in Atlantis queue following ORIF surgery for a hip fracture.
The home health agency's intake coordinator is reported as out sick, leaving the referral in a 'Pending' status without an assigned nurse.
The family expresses significant concern regarding the lack of a confirmed physical therapy schedule for the patient's hip recovery.
Social Worker confirms a backup coordinator at the agency has accepted the case and provided a start-of-care window.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's phone number on the face sheet is inconsistent with the family's verbal report.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the correct contact information with the daughter and documented the update in Atlantis.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA intake coordinator is unavailable, stalling the confirmation of the first home visit.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the HHA acceptance stall to the Social Worker in Atlantis and waited for a status update.

format_2_triples.situation: The family is confused about why a medical assistant is visiting in addition to a home health nurse.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the program benefits and the specific role of the medical assistant in post-surgical monitoring.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to update the Atlantis portal with the HHA's acceptance status without further prompting, acknowledging that the coordinator's illness is outside of the Navigator's control.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the professional boundary by allowing the Social Worker to handle all agency-side logistics while the Navigator remains in a supportive, observational role.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the family's current sentiment score in light of the delay, flag the rising anxiety to the Social Worker, and educate the family on the 'Wait' status to maintain trust in the transition process.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: The Navigator acts as a liaison between the family's concerns and the Social Worker's logistics, ensuring the patient remains engaged without overstepping into vendor management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the home health agency's main office to speak with a supervisor or a different intake staff member to expedite the acceptance of the hip fracture case so the MA visit can be scheduled immediately.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: While intended to help the patient, this violates the No-Vendor Rule and the Verb Filter by attempting to 'solve' a logistical problem that belongs to the Social Worker.
